evidence_quotes.quote: In almost all cases, the exact end date is not communicated before the sale.
Moreover, these sales are very misleading as they appear to buy a product and not a service.
the preservation of purchased video games under the Digital Fairness Act.

paragraphs.text: Dear, I would like to propose the preservation of purchased video games under the Digital Fairness Act. At present, it is possible for companies to sell video games as products and to end them like services, thereby effectively depriving the buyer of the use of the paid video games. In almost all cases, the exact end date is not communicated before the sale. Moreover, these sales are very misleading as they appear to buy a product and not a service. In general, the practice of destroying video games is very problematic, without any possibility of preservation, since, in my view, video games are a kind of art and therefore have a cultural added value that should be preserved for the posterior.
